overview: This W/U/G Infect deck leverages rapid token generation and the infect mechanic to race opponents in Legacy. By combining Glistener Elf and Noble Hierarch, it floods the board with poison-dealing creatures while maintaining mana flexibility. Legolas's Quick Reflexes enables graveyard recursion and surprise attacks, pressuring opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Accumulating ten poison counters on an opponent through infect creature combat damage
Swarming the board with multiple infect tokens to overwhelm blockers
Replaying key threats from the graveyard using Legolas's Quick Reflexes to close out games

keep_criteria: Hands containing at least two lands and a token generator
Early access to Glistener Elf or Noble Hierarch
Presence of Legolas's Quick Reflexes to enable graveyard value
Keep hands with 1-2 lands, at least one 1-drop (Glistener Elf, Noble Hierarch, or Legolas's Quick Reflexes), and a pump spell or discard.
You need a turn-1 playable threat or mana source to execute the game plan.
mulligan_criteria: No creature spells or token producers
Heavy reliance on late-game cards without early pressure
Mulligan if you have zero 1-drops, three or more lands, or only 2-drops with no acceleration.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and token generators to establish board presence
Use Glistener Elf to create infect tokens and apply immediate poison pressure
Hold up mana to protect key threats from early removal
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Noble Hierarch or Glistener Elf.
If you have Legolas's Quick Reflexes, play it for immediate haste and +1/+1.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Kongming's Confidant or Sylvan Caryatid, or pump your 1-drop.
Attack with infect creatures immediately, prioritizing the face for poison.
Sequence mana to ensure you can cast threats and answers on curve without flooding.
mid_game: Leverage Noble Hierarch for mana ramp and additional flying tokens
Activate Legolas's Quick Reflexes to cast creatures from the graveyard on opponent's turn
Trade efficiently to preserve infect tokens while advancing poison counters
Treat mana as a resource for threats and answers, not for storing value.
Keep a pump spell or counterspell in hand to protect your poison strategy.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if you lack answers.
Your graveyard is generally a liability against hate, so prioritize board presence over recursion.
Commit early threats to apply poison pressure and force difficult blocks.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, hard removal, or counterspells they can activate.
late_game: Flood the board with multiple infect sources to bypass blockers
Use graveyard recursion to replay lethal threats repeatedly
Maintain mana advantage through Noble Hierarch to outvalue control strategies
Win by dealing 10 poison counters through combat.
Achieve this by pumping infect creatures, attacking with multiple threats, or using pump spells to break through blockers.
Ensure you have enough damage to close the game before the opponent can stabilize or race you back.
Go all-in when you have two or more infect creatures, a pump spell, and the opponent has no sweepers or hard removal.
Also commit when you're at 5+ poison and the opponent is at low life or has no answers.
Use discard to remove key answers before attacking.

key_cards.name: Glistener Elf
key_cards.notes: Tap for green mana to create 1/1 insect tokens with infect; prioritize keeping it protected to maintain poison pressure

key_cards.name: Legolas's Quick Reflexes
key_cards.notes: Enables casting creature cards from the graveyard with flash; use on opponent's turn to surprise or replay lethal threats

key_cards.name: Noble Hierarch
key_cards.notes: Provides W/U/G mana and creates flying spirit tokens; use to accelerate into larger threats or block while advancing poison

sequencing_tips: Hold up white mana to protect infect tokens from targeted removal
Cast Legolas's Quick Reflexes before playing lands to maximize surprise value
Prioritize attacking with infect creatures over trading to accumulate poison counters
Use Noble Hierarch's flying tokens to block flying threats while keeping ground infect safe
Common mistakes include overextending into sweepers, forgetting to count poison counters, and mismanaging pump spells.
Always track the opponent's life total versus your poison damage.
Legolas's Quick Reflexes can be pumped for immediate lethal, so sequence it carefully.
Fast mana enables turn-2 poison, but don't sacrifice consistency for speed.
Watch for opponent's mana development and adjust your aggression based on their available answers.

matchups.advice: Race efficiently by prioritizing poison counters over board control; use flying tokens to block and Legolas to replay threats.
Apply relentless early pressure to prevent them from stabilizing; use graveyard recursion to bypass counterspells.
Disrupt their casting sequence with early poison pressure; use Legolas to replay threats during their turn to interrupt combos.
Outpace their value generation by flooding the board with infect tokens; avoid trading into their efficient removal.

Use discard to remove counterspells before casting threats. Cast threats in response to counterspells if possible, or use pump spells to make threats too large to counter. Attack with multiple threats to force multiple counterspells. If they have only cheap counters, pump your creatures to bypass them.

Deploy hate cards like Rest in Peace and Gaddock Teeg early to disrupt their mana. Apply pressure before they stabilize. Use targeted removal on key mana sources. If they ramp past your hate, use counterspells and pump spells to race or stabilize.

It's a race to 10 poison. Prioritize faster mana, better pump spells, and hate cards against each other's threats. Win by out-resourcing, applying poison faster, and using discard to remove their answers. Track their poison counters and adjust your aggression accordingly.

common_threats: Board wipes that clear token swarms
Targeted removal for Glistener Elf and Noble Hierarch
Counterspells that disrupt Legolas's Quick Reflexes recursion
Hand disruption that limits early token generation
Watch for Tron's mana rocks, Sneak & Show's graveyard, Delver's flip condition, Ocelot Pride's counters, and Dimir Tempo's counterspells.
Key tells include tapping for multiple colors, drawing extra cards, protecting specific permanents, or holding up mana on your turn.
Adjust your aggression based on their available answers.
